per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA states that bodies 'use the data on their own responsibility,' while the REG concerns safeguards in administrative arrangements subject to supervisory authorisation; these do not clearly express the same constraint or a direct conflict.

The REA addresses use of data by bodies on their own responsibility, whereas the REG permits public authorities or bodies to provide appropriate safeguards by a legally binding instrument; no quote-supported mismatch on the same constraint is clear.

The REA describes data use responsibility, while the REG generally allows appropriate safeguards to be provided subject to supervisory authorisation; the texts are not clearly comparable as the same or conflicting obligation.
